title: Permutation
short: An ordered arrangement of objects. The number of ways to arrange r of n distinct objects is nPr = n!/(n−r)!.
full:
A permutation is a selection in which order matters — first, second and third place are different outcomes. The number of permutations of r objects chosen from n distinct objects is nPr = n!/(n−r)!.

Use permutations when the positions are distinguishable (seating, finishing order, passwords). If all n objects are arranged, the count is simply n!. When order does NOT matter, use combinations instead.
